$comment: Reference A2A Agent Card for the agent-leash educational project. This is a REFERENCE card: there is no live A2A endpoint behind it yet. It exists to show the shape of a standards-aware capability declaration. See docs/a2a-agent-card.md.
name: LEASH-8 Scorecard Agent
description: Educational agent for the LEASH-8 control model: explains the 8 control domains for AI agents with delegated authority, walks an architecture through the scorecard, and returns a scored, evidence-oriented readout. It does not execute actions, hold credentials, or connect to your infrastructure: input is your description, output is text.

skills.name: LEASH-8 scorecard walkthrough
skills.description: Given a plain-text description of an agent system (tools, credentials, approval flow, egress channels), returns a domain-by-domain LEASH-8 score with the evidence each score would require and the cheapest next control to implement.
examples: Score my setup: one Claude agent with shell, browser and Telegram, running under my user account, approvals are me saying ok in the same chat.
Which LEASH-8 domain should a two-person team close first?

skills.name: Approval design review
skills.description: Reviews a described approval flow against the approval-design checklist (out-of-band channel, allowlisted approver, token bound to request id, expiry, deny-path test) and lists which checks fail.
examples: My agent asks for a thumbs-up in Slack before sending invoices. What fails?

$signatureNote: Unsigned. When a live endpoint ships, this card gets a signed version per A2A signed Agent Cards; an unsigned card behind HTTPS on a controlled repo is the honest interim.
